events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: wrong-part-similar-part (ID from bin photo). Work is in-progress. Standard work execution with quality gates. A wrong but similar part was selected from a bin. This is a non-compliant condition - wrong part installed or being installed. Need to fail because non-compliant. Risk medium-high. Component condition: non-compliant, possibly installed-defective if installed. Since in-progress, likely being installed. Let me produce JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 35,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Wrong-but-similar part pulled from bin and being assembled per bin photo ID mismatch",
    "Part does not match standard work BOM/part number for this station",
    "Quality gate not engaged before installation; defect escaped to assembly"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work at the affected station immediately",
    "Quarantine the wrong part and tag bin for verification/replenishment",
    "Verify correct part number against standard work and BOM before resuming",
    "Inspect any assemblies already built with parts from the suspect bin",
    "Notify team lead/quality and log nonconformance per site procedure"
  ],
  "rationale": "A similar-looking but incorrect part is a classic bin-mix error. Under standard work and TWI, the operator must build to the specified part number and quality gates must catch mismatches before installation. Proceeding is non-compliant and risks latent defects downstream, so work must fail and be corrected before continuing.",
  "references": ["site standard work", "TWI"]
}
